spec_trace.roadmap_pointer: Audit remediation against Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.139.md (drop-back smart behavior)

draft_scope.intent:
- What: Implement DropBackStrategy::Smart decision logic (ShouldDropBack) per current spec and ensure it is test-covered.
- Why: Drop-back semantics control how quickly execution returns to base escalation level after success; incorrect behavior creates cost/safety drift.
draft_scope.scope_sketch:
- IN_SCOPE:
  - Implement smart drop-back decision function using last escalation record + policy.
  - Add targeted unit tests for always/never/smart and edge cases.
- OUT_OF_SCOPE:
  - Redesign of the escalation chain itself (separate stub if needed).
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria: - Smart drop-back behaves per spec across representative scenarios and has explicit test coverage.
draft_scope.risks_unknowns: - If spec is ambiguous, require explicit acceptance examples in refinement before implementation.

assumptions_to_attack: A stub is planning metadata, not execution authority.
Stub Markdown may contain stale draft scope until activated through a signed packet.
Apps must not treat stub contracts as runnable work packets.
minimum_controls: Require official packet contract before Coder or Validator launch.
Fail drift checks when stub Markdown changes without contract regeneration.
